heading: Deductions limited to amount at risk
label: 465 Deductions limited to amount at risk

(c)(3)(A). substituted “This” for “In the case of taxable years beginning after , this”.Pub. L. 113–295December 31, 19782004—Subsec. (c)(7)(B). inserted “or” at end of cl. (i), redesignated cl. (D) which read as follows: “In the case of activities described in subparagraph (A), the holding of real property (other than mineral property) shall be treated as a separate activity, and subsection (a) shall not apply to losses from such activity. L. 99–514, title V, § 503(c)100 Stat. 2244Oct. 22, 1986“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in this subsection, the amendments made by this section [amending this section] shall apply to losses incurred after , with respect to property placed in service by the taxpayer after .December 31, 1986December 31, 1986“(2).—Special rule for losses of s corporation, partnership, or pass-thru entityIn the case of an interest in an S corporation, a partnership, or other pass-thru entity acquired after , the amendments made by this section shall apply to losses after , which are attributable to property placed in service by the S corporation, partnership, or pass-thru entity on, before, or after .December 31, 1986December 31, 1986January 1, 1986“(3).—Special rule for athletic stadiumThe amendments made by this section shall not apply to any losses incurred by a taxpayer with respect to the holding of a multi-use athletic stadium in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, which the taxpayer acquired in a sale for which a letter of understanding was entered into before .”April 16, 1986Amendment by applicable to taxable years beginning after , see , set out as a note under .section 1011(b)(1) of Pub. Effective Date and Transitional Rules, , , as amended by , , ; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 94–455, title II, § 204(c)90 Stat. 1532Pub. L. 95–600, title VII, § 701(k)(1)92 Stat. 2906Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Oct. 4, 1976Nov. 6, 1978Oct. 22, 1986“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in paragraphs (2) and (3), the amendments made by this section [enacting this section] shall apply to losses attributable to amounts paid or incurred in taxable years beginning after . For purposes of this subsection, any amount allowed or allowable for depreciation or amortization for any period shall be treated as an amount paid or incurred in such period.December 31, 1975“(2)Special transitional rules for movies and video tapes.—“(A).—In generalIn the case of any activity described in section 465(c)(1)(A) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954], the amendments made by this section shall not apply to—“(i)deductions for depreciation or amortization with respect to property the principal production of which began before , and for the purchase of which there was on , and at all times thereafter a binding contract, andSeptember 11, 1975September 11, 1975“(ii)deductions attributable to producing or distributing property the principal production of which began before .September 11, 1975“(B).—Exception for certain agreements where principal photography begin before 1976In the case of any activity described in section 465(c)(1)(A) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, the amendments made by this section shall not apply to deductions attributable to the producing of a film the principal photography of which began on or before , if—December 31, 1975“(i)on , there was an agreement with the director or a principal motion picture star, or on or before , there had been expended (or committed to the production) an amount not less than the lower of $100,000 or 10 percent of the estimated costs of producing the film, andSeptember 10, 1975September 10, 1975“(ii)the production takes place in the United States.Subparagraph (A) shall apply only to taxpayers who held their interests on . Subparagraph (B) shall apply only to taxpayers who held their interests on .September 10, 1975December 31, 1975“(3)Special transitional rules for leasing activities.—“(A).—Rule for leases other than operating leasesIn the case of any activity described in section 465(c)(1)(C) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, the amendments made by this section shall not apply with respect to—“(i)leases entered into before , andJanuary 1, 1976“(ii)leases where the property was ordered by the lessor or lessee before .January 1, 1976“(B).—Holding of interests for purposes of subparagraph (a)Subparagraph (A) shall apply only to taxpayers who held their interests in the property on .December 31, 1975“(C).—Special rule for operating leasesIn the case of a lease described in section 46(e)(3)(B) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986—“(i)subparagraph (A) shall be applied by substituting ‘’ for ‘’ each place it appears therein, andMay 1, 1976January 1, 1976“(ii)subparagraph (B) shall be applied by substituting ‘’ for ‘’.”April 30, 1976December 31, 1975

Transitional Rules for Recapture Provisions and Leasing Activities, , , as amended by , , ; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 95–600, title II, § 204(b)92 Stat. 2817Pub. L. 96–222, title I, § 102(a)(1)(E)94 Stat. 208Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Nov. 6, 1978Apr. 1, 1980Oct. 22, 1986“(1).—Recapture provisionsIf the amount for which the taxpayer is at risk in any activity as of the close of the taxpayer’s last taxable year beginning before , is less than zero, section 465(e)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] (as added by section 203 of this Act) shall be applied with respect to such activity of the taxpayer by substituting such negative amount for zero.January 1, 1979“(2)Special transitional rules for leasing activities.—“(A).—Rule for leasesIn the case of any activity described in section 465(c)(1)(C) of such Code in which a corporation described in section 465(a)(1)(C) of such Code is engaged, the amendments made by this subtitle [amending sections 465 and 704 of this title and enacting provisions set out as notes under sections 465 and 704 of this title] shall not apply with respect to—“(i)leases entered into before , andNovember 1, 1978“(ii)leases where the property was ordered by the lessor or lessee before .November 1, 1978“(B).—Holding of interests for purposes of subparagraph (a)Subparagraph (A) shall apply only to taxpayers who held their interests in the property on .”October 31, 1978

For purposes of this subsection, any amount allowed or allowable for depreciation or amortization for any period shall be treated as an amount paid or incurred in such period.December 31, 1975“(2)Special transitional rules for movies and video tapes.—“(A).—In generalIn the case of any activity described in section 465(c)(1)(A) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954], the amendments made by this section shall not apply to—“(i)deductions for depreciation or amortization with respect to property the principal production of which began before , and for the purchase of which there was on , and at all times thereafter a binding contract, andSeptember 11, 1975September 11, 1975“(ii)deductions attributable to producing or distributing property the principal production of which began before .September 11, 1975“(B).—Exception for certain agreements where principal photography begin before 1976In the case of any activity described in section 465(c)(1)(A) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, the amendments made by this section shall not apply to deductions attributable to the producing of a film the principal photography of which began on or before , if—December 31, 1975“(i)on , there was an agreement with the director or a principal motion picture star, or on or before , there had been expended (or committed to the production) an amount not less than the lower of $100,000 or 10 percent of the estimated costs of producing the film, andSeptember 10, 1975September 10, 1975“(ii)the production takes place in the United States.Subparagraph (A) shall apply only to taxpayers who held their interests on . Subparagraph (B) shall apply only to taxpayers who held their interests on .September 10, 1975December 31, 1975“(3)Special transitional rules for leasing activities.—“(A).—Rule for leases other than operating leasesIn the case of any activity described in section 465(c)(1)(C) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, the amendments made by this section shall not apply with respect to—“(i)leases entered into before , andJanuary 1, 1976“(ii)leases where the property was ordered by the lessor or lessee before .January 1, 1976“(B).—Holding of interests for purposes of subparagraph (a)Subparagraph (A) shall apply only to taxpayers who held their interests in the property on .December 31, 1975“(C).—Special rule for operating leasesIn the case of a lease described in section 46(e)(3)(B) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986—“(i)subparagraph (A) shall be applied by substituting ‘’ for ‘’ each place it appears therein, andMay 1, 1976January 1, 1976“(ii)subparagraph (B) shall be applied by substituting ‘’ for ‘’.”April 30, 1976December 31, 1975

L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Nov. 6, 1978Apr. 1, 1980Oct. 22, 1986“(1).—Recapture provisionsIf the amount for which the taxpayer is at risk in any activity as of the close of the taxpayer’s last taxable year beginning before , is less than zero, section 465(e)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] (as added by section 203 of this Act) shall be applied with respect to such activity of the taxpayer by substituting such negative amount for zero.January 1, 1979“(2)Special transitional rules for leasing activities.—“(A).—Rule for leasesIn the case of any activity described in section 465(c)(1)(C) of such Code in which a corporation described in section 465(a)(1)(C) of such Code is engaged, the amendments made by this subtitle [amending sections 465 and 704 of this title and enacting provisions set out as notes under sections 465 and 704 of this title] shall not apply with respect to—“(i)leases entered into before , andNovember 1, 1978“(ii)leases where the property was ordered by the lessor or lessee before .November 1, 1978“(B).—Holding of interests for purposes of subparagraph (a)Subparagraph (A) shall apply only to taxpayers who held their interests in the property on .”October 31, 1978

children.heading: Limitation to amount at risk
children.label: a Limitation to amount at risk
A
children.text: an individual, and

children.label: B
children.text: a C corporation with respect to which the stock ownership requirement of paragraph (2) of section 542(a) is met,

children.heading: Deduction in succeeding year
children.label: 2 Deduction in succeeding year
children.text: Any loss from an activity to which this section applies not allowed under this section for the taxable year shall be treated as a deduction allocable to such activity in the first succeeding taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: section 544(a)(2) shall be applied as if such section did not contain the phrase “or by or for his partner”; and

children.label: B
children.text: sections 544(a)(4)(A) and 544(b)(1) shall be applied by substituting “the corporation meet the stock ownership requirements of section 542(a)(2)” for “the corporation a personal holding company”.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of money and the adjusted basis of other property contributed by the taxpayer to the activity, and

children.label: B
children.text: amounts borrowed with respect to such activity (as determined under paragraph (2)).

children.label: A
children.text: is personally liable for the repayment of such amounts, or

children.label: B
children.text: has pledged property, other than property used in such activity, as security for such borrowed amount (to the extent of the net fair market value of the taxpayer’s interest in such property).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except to the extent provided in regulations, for purposes of paragraph (1)(B), amounts borrowed shall not be considered to be at risk with respect to an activity if such amounts are borrowed from any person who has an interest in such activity or from a related person to a person (other than the taxpayer) having such an interest.

children.heading: Interest as creditor
children.label: i Interest as creditor
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to an interest as a creditor in the activity.

children.heading: Interest as shareholder with respect to amounts borrowed by corporation
children.label: ii Interest as shareholder with respect to amounts borrowed by corporation
children.text: In the case of amounts borrowed by a corporation from a shareholder, subparagraph (A) shall not apply to an interest as a shareholder.

children.label: i
children.text: the related person bears a relationship to such person specified in section 267(b) or section 707(b)(1), or

children.label: ii
children.text: the related person and such person are engaged in trades or business under common control (within the meaning of subsections (a) and (b) of section 52).

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 4 Exception
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, a taxpayer shall not be considered at risk with respect to amounts protected against loss through nonrecourse financing, guarantees, stop loss agreements, or other similar arrangements.

children.heading: Amounts at risk in subsequent years
children.label: 5 Amounts at risk in subsequent years
children.text: If in any taxable year the taxpayer has a loss from an activity to which subsection (a) applies, the amount with respect to which a taxpayer is considered to be at risk (within the meaning of subsection (b)) in subsequent taxable years with respect to that activity shall be reduced by that portion of the loss which (after the application of subsection (a)) is allowable as a deduction.

children.heading: Qualified nonrecourse financing treated as amount at risk
children.label: 6 Qualified nonrecourse financing treated as amount at risk

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subsection, in the case of an activity of holding real property, a taxpayer shall be considered at risk with respect to the taxpayer’s share of any qualified nonrecourse financing which is secured by real property used in such activity.

children.label: i
children.text: which is borrowed by the taxpayer with respect to the activity of holding real property,

children.label: ii
children.text: which is borrowed by the taxpayer from a qualified person or represents a loan from any Federal, State, or local government or instrumentality thereof, or is guaranteed by any Federal, State, or local government,

children.label: iii
children.text: except to the extent provided in regulations, with respect to which no person is personally liable for repayment, and

children.label: iv
children.text: which is not convertible debt.

children.heading: Special rule for partnerships
children.label: C Special rule for partnerships
children.text: In the case of a partnership, a partner’s share of any qualified nonrecourse financing of such partnership shall be determined on the basis of the partner’s share of liabilities of such partnership incurred in connection with such financing (within the meaning of section 752).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “qualified person” has the meaning given such term by section 49(a)(1)(D)(iv).

children.heading: Certain commercially reasonable financing from related persons
children.label: ii Certain commercially reasonable financing from related persons
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), section 49(a)(1)(D)(iv) shall be applied without regard to subclause (I) thereof (relating to financing from related persons) if the financing from the related person is commercially reasonable and on substantially the same terms as loans involving unrelated persons.

children.heading: Activity of holding real property
children.label: E Activity of holding real property

children.heading: Incidental personal property and services
children.label: i Incidental personal property and services
children.text: The activity of holding real property includes the holding of personal property and the providing of services which are incidental to making real property available as living accommodations.

children.heading: Mineral property
children.label: ii Mineral property
children.text: The activity of holding real property shall not include the holding of mineral property.

children.heading: Activities to which section applies
children.label: c Activities to which section applies
A
children.text: holding, producing, or distributing motion picture films or video tapes,

children.label: B
children.text: farming (as defined in section 464(e)),

children.label: C
children.text: leasing any section 1245 property (as defined in section 1245(a)(3)),

children.label: D
children.text: exploring for, or exploiting, oil and gas resources, or

children.label: E
children.text: exploring for, or exploiting, geothermal deposits (as defined in section 613(e)(2))1So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.1

children.label: i
children.text: film or video tape,

children.label: ii
children.text: section 1245 property which is leased or held for leasing,

children.label: iii
children.text: farm,

children.label: iv
children.text: oil and gas property (as defined under section 614), or

children.label: v
children.text: geothermal property (as defined under section 614),

children.heading: Special rule for leases of section 1245 property by partnerships or S corporations
children.label: i Special rule for leases of section 1245 property by partnerships or S corporations
I
children.text: are leased or held for lease, and

children.label: II
children.text: are placed in service in any taxable year of the partnership or S corporation,

children.heading: Other aggregation rules
children.label: ii Other aggregation rules
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of subparagraphs (B) and (C) of paragraph (3) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: engaged in by the taxpayer in carrying on a trade or business or for the production of income, and

children.label: ii
children.text: which is not described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Aggregation of activities where taxpayer actively participates in management of trade or business
children.label: B Aggregation of activities where taxpayer actively participates in management of trade or business
i
children.text: the taxpayer actively participates in the management of such trade or business, or

children.label: ii
children.text: such trade or business is carried on by a partnership or an S corporation and 65 percent or more of the losses for the taxable year is allocable to persons who actively participate in the management of the trade or business.

children.heading: Aggregation or separation of activities under regulations
children.label: C Aggregation or separation of activities under regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe regulations under which activities described in subparagraph (A) shall be aggregated or treated as separate activities.

children.heading: Application of subsection (b)(3)
children.label: D Application of subsection (b)(3)
children.text: In the case of an activity described in subparagraph (A), subsection (b)(3) shall apply only to the extent provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Exclusion for certain equipment leasing by closely-held corporations
children.label: 4 Exclusion for certain equipment leasing by closely-held corporations
i
children.text: the activity of equipment leasing shall be treated as a separate activity, and

children.label: ii
children.text: subsection (a) shall not apply to losses from such activity.

children.heading: 50-percent gross receipts test
children.label: B 50-percent gross receipts test
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), a corporation shall not be considered to be actively engaged in equipment leasing unless 50 percent or more of the gross receipts of the corporation for the taxable year is attributable, under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, to equipment leasing.

children.heading: Component members of controlled group treated as a single corporation
children.label: C Component members of controlled group treated as a single corporation
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the component members of a controlled group of corporations shall be treated as a single corporation.

children.heading: Waiver of controlled group rule where there is substantial leasing activity
children.label: 5 Waiver of controlled group rule where there is substantial leasing activity
i
children.text: by substituting “80 percent” for “50 percent” in subparagraph (B) thereof, and

children.label: ii
children.text: as if paragraph (4) did not include subparagraph (C) thereof.

children.heading: At least 3 employees
children.label: i At least 3 employees
children.text: During the entire year, the group had at least 3 full-time employees substantially all of the services of whom were services directly related to the equipment leasing activity of the qualified leasing members.

children.heading: At least 5 separate leasing transactions
children.label: ii At least 5 separate leasing transactions
children.text: During the year, the qualified leasing members in the aggregate entered into at least 5 separate equipment leasing transactions.

children.heading: At least $1,000,000 equipment leasing receipts
children.label: iii At least $1,000,000 equipment leasing receipts
children.text: During the year, the qualified leasing members in the aggregate had at least $1,000,000 in gross receipts from equipment leasing.

children.label: i
children.text: it is a component member of the controlled group of corporations, and

children.label: ii
children.text: it meets the requirements of paragraph (4)(B) (as modified by subparagraph (A)(i) of this paragraph).

children.label: i
children.text: the leasing of equipment which is section 1245 property, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the purchasing, servicing, and selling of such equipment.

children.heading: Leasing of master sound recordings, etc., excluded
children.label: B Leasing of master sound recordings, etc., excluded
children.text: The term “equipment leasing” does not include the leasing of master sound recordings, and other similar contractual arrangements with respect to tangible or intangible assets associated with literary, artistic, or musical properties.

children.heading: Controlled group of corporations; component member
children.label: C Controlled group of corporations; component member
children.text: The terms “controlled group of corporations” and “component member” have the same meanings as when used in section 1563. The determination of the taxable years taken into account with respect to any controlled group of corporations shall be made in a manner consistent with the manner set forth in section 1563.

children.heading: Exclusion of active businesses of qualified C corporations
children.label: 7 Exclusion of active businesses of qualified C corporations
i
children.text: each qualifying business carried on by such taxpayer shall be treated as a separate activity, and

children.label: ii
children.text: subsection (a) shall not apply to losses from such business.

children.label: i
children.text: a personal holding company (as defined in section 542(a)), or

children.label: ii
children.text: a personal service corporation (as defined in section 269A(b) but determined by substituting “5 percent” for “10 percent” in section 269A(b)(2)).

children.label: i
children.text: during the entire 12-month period ending on the last day of the taxable year, such corporation had at least 1 full-time employee substantially all the services of whom were in the active management of such business,

children.label: ii
children.text: during the entire 12-month period ending on the last day of the taxable year, such corporation had at least 3 full-time, nonowner employees substantially all of the services of whom were services directly related to such business,

children.label: iii
children.text: the amount of the deductions attributable to such business which are allowable to the taxpayer solely by reason of sections 162 and 404 for the taxable year exceeds 15 percent of the gross income from such business for such year, and

children.label: iv
children.text: such business is not an excluded business.

children.heading: Special rules for application of subparagraph (C)
children.label: D Special rules for application of subparagraph (C)

children.heading: Partnerships in which taxpayer is a qualified corporate partner
children.label: i Partnerships in which taxpayer is a qualified corporate partner
I
children.text: the taxpayer is a qualified corporate partner in the partnership, and

children.label: II
children.text: during the entire 12-month period ending on the last day of the partnership’s taxable year, there was at least 1 full-time employee of the partnership (or of a qualified corporate partner) substantially all the services of whom were in the active management of such business,

children.label: I
children.text: such corporation is a general partner in the partnership,

children.label: II
children.text: such corporation has an interest of 10 percent or more in the profits and losses of the partnership, and

children.label: III
children.text: such corporation has contributed property to the partnership in an amount not less than the lesser of $500,000 or 10 percent of the net worth of the corporation.

children.heading: Deduction for owner employee compensation not taken into account
children.label: iii Deduction for owner employee compensation not taken into account
children.text: For purposes of clause (iii) of subparagraph (C), there shall not be taken into account any deduction in respect of compensation for personal services rendered by any employee (other than a non-owner employee) of the taxpayer or any member of such employee’s family (within the meaning of section 318(a)(1)).

children.label: I
children.text: gross income shall be determined without regard to the exclusion of interest from gross income under section 103, and

children.label: II
children.text: in addition to the deductions described in such clause, there shall also be taken into account the amount of the deductions which are allowable for amounts paid or credited to the accounts of depositors or holders of accounts as dividends or interest on their deposits or withdrawable accounts under section 163 or 591.

children.heading: Special rule for life insurance companies
children.label: v Special rule for life insurance companies

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Clause (iii) of subparagraph (C) shall not apply to any insurance business of a qualified life insurance company.

children.heading: Insurance business
children.label: II Insurance business
children.text: For purposes of subclause (I), the term “insurance business” means any business which is not a noninsurance business (within the meaning of section 453B(e)(3)).

children.heading: Qualified life insurance company
children.label: III Qualified life insurance company
children.text: For purposes of subclause (I), the term “qualified life insurance company” means any company which would be a life insurance company as defined in section 816 if unearned premiums were not taken into account under subsections (a)(2) and (c)(2) of section 816.

children.heading: Non-owner employee
children.label: i Non-owner employee
children.text: The term “non-owner employee” means any employee who does not own, at any time during the taxable year, more than 5 percent in value of the outstanding stock of the taxpayer. For purposes of the preceding sentence, section 318 shall apply, except that “5 percent” shall be substituted for “50 percent” in section 318(a)(2)(C).

children.label: I
children.text: equipment leasing (as defined in paragraph (6)), and

children.label: II
children.text: any business involving the use, exploitation, sale, lease, or other disposition of master sound recordings, motion picture films, video tapes, or tangible or intangible assets associated with literary, artistic, musical, or similar properties.

children.heading: Special rules relating to communications industry, etc.
children.label: iii Special rules relating to communications industry, etc.

children.heading: Business not excluded where taxpayer not completely at risk
children.label: I Business not excluded where taxpayer not completely at risk
children.text: A business involving the use, exploitation, sale, lease, or other disposition of property described in subclause (II) of clause (ii) shall not constitute an excluded business by reason of such subclause if the taxpayer is at risk with respect to all amounts paid or incurred (or chargeable to capital account) in such business.

children.heading: Certain licensed businesses not excluded
children.label: II Certain licensed businesses not excluded
children.text: For purposes of subclause (II) of clause (ii), the provision of radio, television, cable television, or similar services pursuant to a license or franchise granted by the Federal Communications Commission or any other Federal, State, or local authority shall not constitute an excluded business by reason of such subclause.

children.heading: Affiliated group treated as 1 taxpayer
children.label: F Affiliated group treated as 1 taxpayer

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (G), the component members of an affiliated group of corporations shall be treated as a single taxpayer.

children.heading: Affiliated group of corporations
children.label: ii Affiliated group of corporations
children.text: The term “affiliated group of corporations” means an affiliated group (as defined in section 1504(a)) which files or is required to file consolidated income tax returns.

children.heading: Component member
children.label: iii Component member
children.text: The term “component member” means an includible corporation (as defined in section 1504) which is a member of the affiliated group.

children.heading: Loss of 1 member of affiliated group may not offset income of personal holding company or personal service corporation
children.label: G Loss of 1 member of affiliated group may not offset income of personal holding company or personal service corporation
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph shall permit any loss of a member of an affiliated group to be used as an offset against the income of any other member of such group which is a personal holding company (as defined in section 542(a)) or a personal service corporation (as defined in section 269A(b) but determined by substituting “5 percent” for “10 percent” in section 269A(b)(2)).

children.heading: Definition of loss
children.label: d Definition of loss
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “loss” means the excess of the deductions allowable under this chapter for the taxable year (determined without regard to the first sentence of subsection (a)) and allocable to an activity to which this section applies over the income received or accrued by the taxpayer during the taxable year from such activity (determined without regard to subsection (e)(1)(A)).

children.heading: Recapture of losses where amount at risk is less than zero
children.label: e Recapture of losses where amount at risk is less than zero
A
children.text: the taxpayer shall include in his gross income for such taxable year (as income from such activity) an amount equal to such excess, and

children.label: B
children.text: an amount equal to the amount so included in gross income shall be treated as a deduction allocable to such activity for the first succeeding taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: the aggregate amount of the reductions required by subsection (b)(5) with respect to the activity by reason of losses for all prior taxable years beginning after , reduced byDecember 31, 1978

children.label: B
children.text: the amounts previously included in gross income with respect to such activity under this subsection.
